column5: Description
column6: Actor (RU = Railway Undertaker, IM = Infrastructure Manager, TA = Ticketing Authority)

column5: Provide information about transports for customers

column4: Publish Passenger Timetable and Route Information
column5: Provide trip planning basic information like timetables and maps for overview information and non-digital trip planning.
Provide digital trip planning tools for mobile and fixed devices.
Provide real-time passenger information in vehicles, in stations and on customer devices.

column4: Inform Freight Customer in real time
column5: Provide real-time information for freight customers about the status of the transport and estimated time of arrival.
Recommend and guide travelers to alternative routes during disruptions

column4: Perform passenger and freight transport operations
column5: Provide services for passengers and goods during, before and after transport. Monitor train operations, vehicles, and infrastructure in operation. Manage disruptions and dispatch in short-term track capacity, rolling stock and staff according to the situation.
Adjustments of the roster due to unplanned short-term staff shortages because an employee cannot start their shift or cannot perform a scheduled task during their shift.
Adjustments of the roster due to unplanned short-term loss of the availability of a vehicle like a train, engine or carriage.
Train driving (e.g. including Driver Advisory System)
Check tickets on the train or before and after boarding or entering the station.
Provide services for customers during the journey on the train as well as before and after boarding the train.

Provide services for the transport of goods during the rail transport as well as before and after the transport. This can include services for safe transportation, loading and unloading as well as custom services.
Coordinate help in an emergency situation. Alarm or dispatch emergency teams and equipment. This includes the operation of emergency trains, firefighters and ambulances.
Monitoring via Sensors, Axles Counter, Signaling, Hotbox, Gage control. Control via Interlocking system.
Monitor rolling stock during operation with sensors on the train and with wayside installations.
Market and sell transport services for passengers and goods according to demand.
Sell transport services to freight customers.
Sell tickets and other additional services to passengers.
Forecast long-term demand for rail traffic and modal split.
Define pricing systems and policies for rail services.
Design, build, operate and maintain infrastructure needed for rail transport.
Design, build, and maintain rail network including track, engineering structures, tunnels, bridges, catenary, signaling, and control technology
Design, build, maintain, and operate passenger facilities
Design, build, maintain, and operate freight facilities including marshalling yards.
Design, build, maintain, and operate energy supply and distribution facilities including buying energy from suppliers.
Provide connectivity for interlocking, communication, and general data transfer.
Design, Operate long term planning to short term planning
Long term timetable and studies. Including timetable planning for long-planned construction and track works
Short term path request, Track Occupancy Diagram
Selling capacity to railway undertakers (long and short term)
Manage marshalling and shunting
Design, build, maintenance and management of rolling stock

Plan and assign work schedules for rolling stock. This involves the creation of a roster which determines when and where vehicles are required and assigned to tasks. This includes the allocation of rolling stock to specific routes and services.
Plan long-term capacity of rolling stock to meet long-term demand.
Predictive maintenance, long term and daily planning maintenance
Procurement of rolling stock by the railway company from the manufacturer
Design and manufacture rolling stock
Plan and prepare personnel for railway-specific tasks. Plan and provide the necessary personnel capacities.

Plan and assign work schedules for personnel. Create a roster which determines when and where an employee is assigned to a task. This includes the allocation of staff to specific routes and services taking into account the necessary knowledge and legal requirements.
Plan long-term demand and supply of personnel capacities in the different personnel categories. Define demand for recruiting and training.

Recruit and train employees in all categories like locomotive drivers, train attendants, signal box personnel, technical maintenance staff for rolling stock and signal technology, as well as personnel for the construction and maintenance of infrastructure. Create and maintain the necessary learning content and tools, including simulators.
Design, build, buy or share software and operate applications. Buy application services.
Provide or buy data center services.
Manage IT security
General management of a company
Human Resources management of a company
Financial management of a company
Ensure that the company complies with applicable laws and regulations while conducting its legal affairs in its best interest
General supply chain management processes.
Manage properties and buildings for corporate needs.
